title: Final judgment; signing; appeals
doc_html:
CHAPTER 3. RENDITION

Art. 1911. Final judgment; signing; appeals

            A. Except as otherwise provided by law, every final judgment shall contain the typewritten or printed name of the judge and be signed by the judge. Any judgment that does not contain the typewritten or printed name of the judge shall not be invalidated for that reason. Judgments may be signed by the judge by use of electronic signature.

            B. For the purpose of an appeal as provided in Article 2083, no appeal shall be taken from a final judgment until the judgment has been signed by the judge.

            Amended by Acts 1974, No. 87, §1; Acts 1979, No. 618, §1; Acts 1999, No. 1263, §1, eff. Jan. 1, 2000; Acts 2014, No. 144, §1; Acts 2014, No. 606, §1; Acts 2023, No. 272, §1; Acts 2025, No. 250, §3.
doc_text:
CHAPTER 3.  RENDITION
Art. 1911.  Final judgment; signing; appeals
A.  Except as otherwise provided by law, every final judgment shall contain the
typewritten or printed name of the judge and be signed by the judge.  Any judgment that does
not contain the typewritten or printed name of the judge shall not be invalidated for that
reason.  Judgments may be signed by the judge by use of electronic signature.
B.  For the purpose of an appeal as provided in Article 2083, no appeal shall be taken
from a final judgment until the judgment has been signed by the judge.
Amended by Acts 1974, No. 87, §1; Acts 1979, No. 618, §1; Acts 1999, No. 1263,
§1, eff. Jan. 1, 2000; Acts 2014, No. 144, §1; Acts 2014, No. 606, §1; Acts 2023, No. 272,
§1; Acts 2025, No. 250, §3.
